name: Jay Inslee
subject: Courage means doing what's hard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
After the horrific violence in Las Vegas, we must act -- together -- to renew the Assault Weapons Ban.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTIwNjY1MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDI1MzQxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Dear Friend, It's difficult for me conceive of the horrific violence suffered in Las Vegas Sunday night. Trudi and I, and all Washingtonians, pray for the hundreds wounded and those fighting now for their lives, and we grieve for the 59 individuals killed. While we pray, we're also in awe of the actions of the first responders who showed incredible courage. And we are inspired by the heroic stories of strangers protecting those near them during the shooting. We draw hope from these stories. One thing I know during these dark chapters in our American story is that our actions define us. We saw that from the first responders and everyday citizens Sunday night. Next, we must see that same courage from the rest of us -- even when it's hard. One critical place to start: Congress should reinstate the Assault Weapons Ban. I voted for that ban as a member of Congress. And when politicians in Washington D.C. allowed the Assault Weapons Ban to expire, it was an egregious abdication of leadership. Now is the time to fix that mistake. Two nights ago in Las Vegas, we saw what American courage and bravery looks like in the face of incredible danger. Now, our representatives in D.C. must show a sliver of that courage and act. [**Today, I 'm joining with millions of Americans to call on Congress to renew the Assault Weapons Ban -- add your name and join the call for action.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIwNjY1MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDI1MzQxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) The vote I took back in 1994 for gun safety reform cost me my seat in Congress, yet I have never once regretted it. I'd take that vote again and again if I could. Yesterday, a few hundred Republican lawmakers tweeted their "thoughts and prayers" for the victims in Las Vegas while many of them took thousands of dollars from the gun lobby for their campaigns. The same gun lobby that fights against all common sense gun safety measures. So let me be clear: In this dark time, we will be judged by our actions. Courage means standing up for what's right, even when it's hard. Even when it means going against a big donor. Last year, after the horrific attack in Orlando, Americans in states including Washington and California voted at the ballot overwhelmingly for common sense gun safety measures. Nevada also passed gun safety legislation by popular vote. But gun violence is a national issue -- it touches Americans in every corner of the country -- and it demands a national solution. Renewing the Assault Weapons Ban is just one step towards common sense gun safety in America. But it's an important step. Actions matter. We saw that in Las Vegas last night and now we must see if from Congress. We pray. We hope. And now we must act. Please join me here: [**jayinslee.com/gunsafety**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTIwNjY1MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDI1MzQxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SIGN ON »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTIwNjY1MTcS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI2MDI1MzQxNQS2&mt=1&rt=0)

body:
This election is an all-hands-on-deck moment for Virginia Democrats.
---
**Sign up now to learn more about how you can help Virginia Democrats win.

** **[I'M IN](http://my.democrats. "http://my.democrats.org/Commit-to-Volunteer-in-Virginia?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+IM+IN&utm_campaign=em_20171004_ke_vav&source=em_20171004_ke_vav")**

Derek, Democrats from all over the country will have their eyes on what happens in Virginia on November 7th.

The DNC is making huge investments there because we know that so much is at stake in this election. Virginia families cannot afford the damage that would be done if Ed Gillespie rolls back all of the progress made under Governor McAuliffe these past four years.

That's why we're seeing grassroots supporters from as far away as California commit to making calls and helping us mobilize the commonwealth in these final five weeks, and I'd like you to be part of it.

**[I'm calling on everyone who can to join us, Derek. Will you sign up today?](http://my.democrats. VA Democrats are counting on you, your family, your friends, and your neighbors to hit the streets in these last 34 days to help make sure that every person who wants to can cast a ballot.

I'm doing everything I can for Ralph Northam, Mark Herring, Justin Fairfax, and the countless amazing Democrats running for Virginia House of Delegates next month, and I hope you will, too.

Keith

name: Bob =?UTF-8?Q?Fertik=2C=20Democrats=2Ecom?=
subject: Join Gabby Giffords: Act to stop gun violence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Americans for Responsible Solutions PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
At this point, we know the feeling of heartbreak and horror too well.

Activist -

At this point, we know the feeling of heartbreak and horror too well. The massacre in Las Vegas, Nevada is a grave tragedy for our nation, the deadliest mass shooting in modern American history.

This must stop. We must stop this.

Our thoughts and prayers are with the victims of this shooting, their families, and their friends. But the truth is, for those who have the power to act and to save lives, thoughts and prayers are not nearly enough.

So today, I am praying for my former colleagues as well -- that they find the courage to make progress on the issue of gun violence in America. I know that many of them got into politics for the same reason that I did, to make a difference, to get things done. I hope that they will.

Some will say that now is not the time to have this conversation, but the truth is that we cannot wait. Congress cannot delay. Now is exactly the right time to take positive action that will keep our our communities safer.

The nation is counting on them.

I also know enough from my time in Congress that action is only possible if people make their voices heard. So today, especially today, I want to ask you to do just that -- to demand action from our elected leaders. Action that will save lives:

Tell Congress: ENOUGH is ENOUGH. Pass legislation to make our communities safer from gun violence. To do nothing is not acceptable. Now is the time for action. (http://action.americansforresponsiblesolutions.org/page/s/enough-dc?source=ads-171004-demscom)

We watch, time and time again, as people describe these mass shootings as unimaginable acts of evil, but the sad fact is that this is not true. There is no other advanced nation in the world where these kinds of mass shootings happen with this kind of frequency.

The only thing that is unimaginable is the continuous legislative inaction on this issue.

Hopefully this time will be different, but I know that's only possible if all of us are willing to act.

All my best,

Gabby Giffords

name: Ronna McDaniel
subject: Las Vegas
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican National Committee
body:
Peter,

President Trump is in Las Vegas paying his respects to the survivors of Sunday night’s senseless tragedy and the heartbroken families of the 59 victims.

It was an act of pure evil that will forever leave a stain on our nation.

But while we saw the worst in mankind that night, we also saw the best. Heroes sacrificed their lives to shield their loved ones and even complete strangers.

As President Trump said, “even the most terrible despair can be illuminated by a single ray of hope.”

Those heroes who gave up everything that night will forever be that ray of hope.

I ask you to keep everyone affected by this heinous act of evil in your thoughts and prayers.

And if you are able to, President Trump and I encourage you to give blood to those battling for their lives as we speak.

You can find out more about donating here: http://click.campaigns.rnchq.com/?qs=19234f22fc32f30e03dd053dccfc977f79fa5703621a0997d7232c0c76afc17c09c76c1016e6a9494d5dc7fa2613c3af

Thank you and God bless America,

Ronna McDaniel

Chairwoman, Republican National Committee

name: Miruxa Cardenas
subject: Tell Congress: Extend the Jones Act exemption
body:
At 5:30 a.m. on Wednesday, September 19, my husband Jose called me from Puerto Rico
#
Friends,
At 5:30 a.m. on Wednesday, September 19, my husband Jose called me from Puerto Rico to tell me our apartment was shaking. As he took shelter in the tub from Hurricane Maria we got disconnected. After five days with no communication from Miami I was fearing the worst--maybe he flew away in the hurricane, was trapped under cement, was hurt and couldn't get help.
When I finally spoke to Jose on Sunday, he told me he was without potable water, the apartment didn't suffer damages, just water and sand. Sand! I live three blocks from the coastal line on the sixth floor. Maria had a lot of fury.
A ship takes five days from Jacksonville to San Juan or any other port in Puerto Rico. If Panama, Venezuela, or our neighbor the Dominican Republic wanted to send in assistance, the Jones Act requires it to dock first in a US port, like Jacksonville, and then head over to Puerto Rico. My husband and many others cannot wait that long for aid.
**[Please tell your member of Congress to extend Puerto Rico's exemption from the Jones Act.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af06/bed43eb/1edbbd82/75ffe3b7/4105610289/VEsH/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/content/jones-act/?source=em171004")**
The line to the supermarket was four streets long, no gasoline, no water, no electricity. One day after five hours on a line to enter the supermarket, when he was getting close to the entrance they closed because they ran out of diesel for the generator.
**[Puerto Rico needs an extended exemption from the Jones Act to survive. Call your member of Congress today and tell them to extend the Jones Act exemption for Puerto Rico.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7af06/bed43eb/1edbbd82/75ffe3b7/4105610289/VEsE/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/content/jones-act/?source=em171004")**
Puerto Rico needs our held. Thank you for taking action.
In solidarity,
Miruxa Cardenas
Our Revolution Puerto Rico

name: The White House
subject: President Trump in Puerto Rico
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=38da6afaced81dc5c035ee1159b67e89ceeb933943056f11aca92004249859a018cfc73c0832f8474bcf2acd1d0b71d9a0e2f44b0a9d39a2

The President and First Lady visit Puerto Rico
President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump traveled to Puerto Rico yesterday to meet with officials from both Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. President Trump delivered remarks at Luis Muñis Air National Guard Base, thanking all the first responders and volunteers participating in rescue and rebuilding efforts. The President then went on a walking tour of the Guaynabo vicinity, where he met with families of the community before making his way to Calvary Chapel Puerto Rico to help distribute supplies to those affected by the devastating storm.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=38da6afaced81dc5b75fb2d0a35d53bd34662ebb1207aa7bc766f77cd879ece9088c570ed3f922a4fc834dafc5514e9bb5bc848d0cfd6421
See More

Hurricane Update
We continue to make progress through the unified efforts of the federal government, working hand-in-hand with Puerto Rico, its municipalities, businesses, and voluntary agencies on the island.

FEMA, working in coordination with federal partners, has provided millions of meals and millions of liters of water to Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Additional meals and water continue to arrive to the islands regularly via air and sea.

Yesterday, the Department of Defense hospital ship, USNS Comfort, arrived in Puerto Rico to provide additional surge capacity if needed. The ship offers the full range of medical care with surgical, obstetric, and pediatric capability and is equivalent to a level-two trauma center in the United States. The ship is staffed by more than 500 medical personnel and support staff and holds 250 hospital beds.

Also yesterday, President Trump amended the disaster declarations for both

Joe Biden has an important message to share about the upcoming special election in Alabama. Doug Jones is counting on us to help him compete against Roy Moore >>
---
**[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171004-MustSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")**
Team --

Joe Biden knows the importance of this Alabama special election -- that's why he went to Birmingham yesterday to speak at a rally for Democratic nominee Doug Jones.

And we wanted to make sure you saw what Joe Biden had to say yesterday:

> _Look, I 've campaigned for a lot of candidates over the years... and I can count on two hands the people I've campaigned for who have as much integrity, courage, and sense of honor and duty that Doug Jones has.
>
> For 40 years justice had gone undone. The 16th Street Baptist Church. He helped remove 40 years of stain and pain. 40 years...Doug said no more. The Klan needed to know that justice would follow them to the gates of hell if need be.
>
> Justice was delayed but it could not be denied. And anyone who knew this man was not at all surprised...Let's get him in the Senate...I'm asking._
Let's stand with Joe and give Doug the strong start he needs in this special election by giving him the resources he needs to take on GOP nominee Roy Moore. Are you in?

[**Chip in $1 right now to answer Joe Biden 's call to action -- and help show the GOP that grassroots Democrats are ALL IN to fight for every seat in 2018.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/take-on-republicans-everywhere?refcode=DPE-20171004-MustSee_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5")

This will be a tough race, there's no doubt about it. But what's important at this moment is that Democrats show we are united in our support for Doug.

The Republicans just went through a bitter primary where ultra-conservative Roy Moore edged out the establishment candidate supported by Mitch McConnell and Donald Trump. Now we have the chance to deal them another blow by electing Doug to the Senate.

Let's show the GOP that Democrats will compete everywhere -- even in Alabama.

**Your contribution will allow Doug to run a competitive campaign against Roy Moore while helping to elect Democratic Senators in races across the country. Will you pitch in today?**

[**http://www.dscc.org/support-Democratic-Senate-candidates**](http://action.dscc. | Dear Peter, I’d like to introduce you to Every Home for Christ. I discovered this ministry last year through its powerful prayer tool _Grace for America,_ a 40-day prayer guide dedicated to the 2016 election season. Since then, we have partnered with Every Home for Christ to bring you a weekly prayer focus to lift up our nation. If you want to receive these updates [sign up here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fpray%2F&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171004_ehc_s6&n=3). At its core, Every Home for Christ believes that Jesus changes everything. For 70 years, this ministry has taken the Gospel to people’s homes and made disciples, from the Brazilian rainforest, to the African bush, to Asian megacities, to right here in the United States. As a result, over 1.9 billion families have received the Gospel, and over 193 million people have responded to Jesus. I can’t think of any organization that is more passionate, more strategic or more inspiring in its approach to obeying the Great Commission. The testimonies streaming in through Every Home for Christ’s work are extraordinary. I encourage you to find out more about what God is doing in the nations [by clicking here](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.ehc.org%2FMFV&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171004_ehc_s6&n=4 "Every Home for Christ"). I think you’ll find it to be uplifting. Faith makes America stronger,

Jason Yates
CEO
My Faith Votes

body:
[ ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=5MdO6cLza2MazVJVOHyurtFG9UotBOmPsT6D1fiWXlI-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MgQQV53R8GYztbznCenTv3v7MZuLQIhCHzCdwMXgXrEktm2-2BTnAk89jHR-2FETVH5RWku-2FdRexJ8qg-2FHn5ZZ-2BMOk1if-2FBzGRQGdQYGpq16mDsEiMdjdNsEhpYlk0mOL-2BAIuNh9BySpeULktXvr7dkullZtxstsG8BrqRgto1pI1yNDtAF3-2BfT9S6DIBOotokcLNXyz0I2IUvIVd-2FYfraiUlpvdIBNkqXfZhEcclNR3KPTJ8hv-2BrVgXy06ncJIAPY3qRPzRdJgD5aeHc-2BP7cvh00hw-3D-3D) [ ](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyBO5kQrx-2FesLRaGJ1fd7TCqcycBI512yYjvguVrkx1y-2Fz9KPysRoLmviHiSY-2BFOWSDCeFAmVCD8YnCFVjty-2FOaIvwADdOammChl-2BIWNAYKSP_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MgQQV53R8GYztbznCenTv3v7MZuLQIhCHzCdwMXgXrEktm2-2BTnAk89jHR-2FETVH5RWku-2FdRexJ8qg-2FHn5ZZ-2BMOk4RbsCdxY67pEy18S55pwku9PHyVYM0VamjYrAgrcN0NhXcNdgdphuKGwN4QsTsdnCz2ht-2FlCa6wNROTalBKtgO2d9HriSdYae9d5lsHt2UuzSeMqQhZ84VHksZcPmDSs0LE-2FZhztCHew1Mz0GSuev9l4GrNxPt7tyq4hYC6RUNRvJe86pBnQC9L-2BObYHPd7uA-3D-3D) Derek,
There is a critical need to get comprehensive tax reform passed in Congress, but the members of our federal delegation do not seem to understand why, after more than 30 years, that now is the time for tax reform.
_[Governor Sununu understands.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyM-2FPW2p3d3iGIo3IbJ5esyfGPEXq8nDfJ20dLS9g43otmxmn9hADtWcsqOsAdPOGtwDM0e49wxpor92mD0xQ6z2CQOJK8eJH96uFxXCGru6fEpQ4kPFDQyj81RdBwKpiNWZY4TVCTbLTFNCEoMvFqJU-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MgQQV53R8GYztbznCenTv3v7MZuLQIhCHzCdwMXgXrEktm2-2BTnAk89jHR-2FETVH5RWku-2FdRexJ8qg-2FHn5ZZ-2BMOk2KwxaOjG2n0XMgD-2BGhwzvNPmYptXUoVGt16JPRFyOmrIRISRFyBVN-2FZ1yxALoOlRF-2F2ngFn0JhQnOwBfwVBnA29fkE-2B2AKJRNcnghckxpFk4mZ11fveI6g6OmywK9DqKDiLLfkh-2FRVbdnewQO-2BNMR0NXLb4S8qqr2wb3TTsVWhIWsxyq9gv-2FN-2BI1s4fo-2FzyFw-3D-3D)___
_[President Trump understands.](htt

name: Team Ryan
subject: Thank you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Ryan, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Ryan for Congress, Inc
body:
****
**** **** | |
| | **October 04, 2017**
---|---

We are all still in shock from the horror that we witnessed in Las Vegas. Many are still fighting for their lives in hospitals and many more are grieving for their loved ones. This pain isn't something that will go away anytime soon. As we recover, we can be thankful for the heroes who stepped up on Sunday night to save their friends and complete strangers and for the first-responders, doctors, and nurses who provided immediate and continued care for the injured. We also thank the Americans who always show what's best about our country, through prayer, donations, or by giving blood.

We as Americans do not let ourselves be defined by tragedy. Whether the tragedies are natural, like the hurricanes that hit the Gulf and Puerto Rico last month, or are the works of evil like in Las Vegas, when we help those who suffer, men and women everywhere become heroes.

| **** | **The AP News**
---
**Acts of heroism emerge in chaos of Las Vegas shooting******

_
LAS VEGAS (AP) -- Rob's battlefield instincts kicked in quickly as bullets rained overhead.

The 42-year-old U.S. Army veteran who served as a sniper in Iraq immediately began tending to the wounded, one of several heroes to emerge from the deadliest mass shooting in modern U.S. history. Amid the massacre in Las Vegas, which left 59 people dead and more than 500 injured, there were acts of compassion and countless heroics that officials say saved scores of lives.

There was a man one survivor knows only as Zach who herded people to a safe place. There was a registered nurse from Tennessee who died shielding his wife.
...

Sonny Melton, a registered nurse, died in the shooting, according to The Henry County Medical Center in Paris, Tennessee, where he worked.

His wife, Dr. Melton, an orthopedic surgeon who was with him when shots were fired, survived. She told WZTV in Nashville, Tennessee, that her husband "saved my life and lost his." She said her husband was the most kind-hearted, loving man she ever met._

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MzA4ODg1MzMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE0MDUxMzU0OAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
****
| **** | **USA Today**
---
**Husband and wife stay in line of fire to check pulses, help victims before paramedics arrive**********

_
PORTLAND, Ore. -- A husband and wife from Marion County stood their ground to help the victims in the mass shooting in Las Vegas.

"It had nothing to do with being a hero," said Dawn-Marie.
...

What they and many others did is nothing short of remarkable, but they will tell you they are not heroes.

"That's being a human being," said Dawn-Marie. "That's doing our job."

It is a lesson they hope to pass down to their children and others.

"The only way this stops is if more people like us do something about it."_

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=9&ms=MzA4ODg1MzMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE0MDUxMzU0OAS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****
| | **CNN**
---
**The Las Vegas heroes who stepped up as chaos reigned**

****
_
An off-duty nurse from Orange County, California, told KTNV that she ran back into the danger to help rescue those who had suffered gunshot wounds.

We went back because I'm a nurse and I just felt that I had to," the woman, who identified herself only as Vanessa, told KTNV.

"I went to three different scenes. The first one was OK. The second one was worse. And by the time I got to the third one, there was just dead bodies.

"There was so many people, just normal citizens, doctors, cops, paramedics, nurses, just off-duty. Everyone was just communicating and working together.

"It was completely horrible, but it was absolutely amazing to see all of those people come together."_

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=6&ms=MzA4ODg1MzMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE0MDUxMzU0OAS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****
| | **CBS News**
---
**Everybody was coming together**
****
_
LAS VEGAS -- Minutes after the shooting at the Las Vegas country concert began, first responders scrambled to the scene.

But some were already there.

Off-duty first responders in the crowd, like Robert with the LA Fire Department, had been attending the concert for fun. He was there with his wife and friends, and went from person to person in the crowd to see how he could help._

[**Read More**](http://links.targetedvictory.mkt5241.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MzA4ODg1MzMS1&r=NDY4NTQ0MTA2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE0MDUxMzU0OAS2&mt=1&rt=0)****
****
| **** | **NBC News**
---
**Blood Donation Lines Stretch for Blocks Following Las Vegas Massacre******

_The mayor of Las Vegas, the governor of Nevada and the Clark County Sheriff all urged people to give blood, while the Las Vegas police department directed them to three locations, two in Las Vegas and one in Henderson.

And it was clear that blood was being donated -- by the bucket load -- across the state.

At United Blood Services, the first donors showed up at around 2 a.m. (5 a.m. ET), staff said -- just a few hours after the 10 p.m. Sunday shooting. By 4 in the afternoon the lines stretched out of the facility, around the front of the building and far back into the parking lot. A small army of volunteers gave out burritos, rice and beans, water and bananas.

Joe, 63, who moved to Las Vegas from Boston around a month ago, got there at 6 a.m. and was still preparing to give blood shortly after 4 p.m. His only regret was that he forgot a book.

"I just thought it was the least I can do," the longtime blood donor said.___

**How to help Las Vegas shooting victims**

_If you live outside Nevada and want to help victims of the Sunday mass-shooting in Las Vegas, you can donate blood or money.

The shooting from a 32nd-story window of the Mandalay Bay hotel into the Route 91 Harvest country music festival left at least 59 people dead and 527 injured. It was the deadliest mass shooting in modern U.S. history.

clean_body: ** | | | | October 04, 2017 ---|--- We are all still in shock from the horror that we witnessed in Las Vegas. Many are still fighting for their lives in hospitals and many more are grieving for their loved ones. This pain isn't something that will go away anytime soon. As we recover, we can be thankful for the heroes who stepped up on Sunday night to save their friends and complete strangers and for the first-responders, doctors, and nurses who provided immediate and continued care for the injured. We also thank the Americans who always show what's best about our country, through prayer, donations, or by giving blood. We as Americans do not let ourselves be defined by tragedy. Whether the tragedies are natural, like the hurricanes that hit the Gulf and Puerto Rico last month, or are the works of evil like in Las Vegas, when we help those who suffer, men and women everywhere become heroes. | | The AP News Acts of heroism emerge in chaos of Las Vegas shooting**** LAS VEGAS (AP) -- Rob's battlefield instincts kicked in quickly as bullets rained overhead. The 42-year-old U.S. Army veteran who served as a sniper in Iraq immediately began tending to the wounded, one of several heroes to emerge from the deadliest mass shooting in modern U.S. history. Amid the massacre in Las Vegas, which left 59 people dead and more than 500 injured, there were acts of compassion and countless heroics that officials say saved scores of lives. There was a man one survivor knows only as Zach who herded people to a safe place. There was a registered nurse from Tennessee who died shielding his wife. ... Sonny Melton, a registered nurse, died in the shooting, according to The Henry County Medical Center in Paris, Tennessee, where he worked. His wife, Dr. Melton, an orthopedic surgeon who was with him when shots were fired, survived. She told WZTV in Nashville, Tennessee, that her husband "saved my life and lost his." She said her husband was the most kind-hearted, loving man she ever met. | | USA Today Husband and wife stay in line of fire to check pulses, help victims before paramedics arrive******** PORTLAND, Ore. -- A husband and wife from Marion County stood their ground to help the victims in the mass shooting in Las Vegas. "It had nothing to do with being a hero," said Dawn-Marie. ... What they and many others did is nothing short of remarkable, but they will tell you they are not heroes. "That's being a human being," said Dawn-Marie. "That's doing our job." It is a lesson they hope to pass down to their children and others. "The only way this stops is if more people like us do something about it." | | CNN The Las Vegas heroes who stepped up as chaos reigned An off-duty nurse from Orange County, California, told KTNV that she ran back into the danger to help rescue those who had suffered gunshot wounds. We went back because I'm a nurse and I just felt that I had to," the woman, who identified herself only as Vanessa, told KTNV. "I went to three different scenes. The first one was OK. The second one was worse. And by the time I got to the third one, there was just dead bodies. "There was so many people, just normal citizens, doctors, cops, paramedics, nurses, just off-duty. Everyone was just communicating and working together. "It was completely horrible, but it was absolutely amazing to see all of those people come together." | | CBS News Everybody was coming together LAS VEGAS -- Minutes after the shooting at the Las Vegas country concert began, first responders scrambled to the scene. But some were already there. Off-duty first responders in the crowd, like Robert with the LA Fire Department, had been attending the concert for fun. He was there with his wife and friends, and went from person to person in the crowd to see how he could help. | | NBC News Blood Donation Lines Stretch for Blocks Following Las Vegas Massacre**** The mayor of Las Vegas, the governor of Nevada and the Clark County Sheriff all urged people to give blood, while the Las Vegas police department directed them to three locations, two in Las Vegas and one in Henderson. And it was clear that blood was being donated -- by the bucket load -- across the state. At United Blood Services, the first donors showed up at around 2 a.m. (5 a.m. ET), staff said -- just a few hours after the 10 p.m. Sunday shooting. By 4 in the afternoon the lines stretched out of the facility, around the front of the building and far back into the parking lot. A small army of volunteers gave out burritos, rice and beans, water and bananas. Joe, 63, who moved to Las Vegas from Boston around a month ago, got there at 6 a.m. and was still preparing to give blood shortly after 4 p.m. His only regret was that he forgot a book. "I just thought it was the least I can do," the longtime blood donor said. | | Bonus: The Detroit Free Press How to help Las Vegas shooting victims If you live outside Nevada and want to help victims of the Sunday mass-shooting in Las Vegas, you can donate blood or money. The shooting from a 32nd-story window of the Mandalay Bay hotel into the Route 91 Harvest country music festival left at least 59 people dead and 527 injured. It was the deadliest mass shooting in modern U.S. history. Peter,

It's almost game time.

We're now just a little over one month out from Election Day 2017, and we have crucial local elections in Virginia and Washington state, where we've endorsed a slate of 25 incredible women.

Now's the last chance to send them a surge of support that could make the difference between a win and a loss.

**[Donate $3 now and support ALL 25 of our candidates running for office in Virginia, Washington state, and across the country.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6188/24c69fd9/8c4161d0/189d6470/669842733/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171004_email_OneMonthToElection?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+now+and+support+ALL+25+of+our+c&utm_campaign=WQEFEJ05N_digital_email_20171004_OneMonthToElection_inact&source=WQEFEJ05N_digital_email_20171004_OneMonthToElection_inact")**

One good thing that came out of the 2016 election: pro-choice Democratic women are showing up in droves to put their names on the ballot.

That's certainly the case in Virginia, where the 2016 election inspired a whole new wave of women to run for office. The women we've endorsed want to help flip the Virginia state House from red to blue, and many are running in districts that went for Hillary Clinton in 2016.

AND we've endorsed six women running in Washington's election for seats in the state legislature, including candidates with long careers fighting for women and families.

During this crunch time before Election Day, it's more important than ever to support all of our women running in Virginia and Washington.

**Donate $3 and help our women win these critical races:**

Lucinda Guinn

body:
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde69/2a380d31/1d6f8181/fe05b0/2564500261/VEsH/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
[](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde69/2a380d31/1d6f8181/fe05b0/2564500261/VEsE/ "http://www.maggiehassan.com/")
Hello,
After trumpeting the baseless claim that millions of people voted illegally in the presidential election, Trump formed a sham voting commission -- the "Commission on Election Integrity."
This is nothing but a shallow attempt to suppress voters, specifically low income voters, the elderly, those who experience disabilities, and people of color.
Sound shady and un-American? It is.
**That 's why we're collecting 10,000 signatures for our petition telling Republicans to STOP attacking voting rights. [Please -- will you sign it?](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde69/2a380d31/1d6f8181/fe05b1/2564500261/VEsF/ "http://www.trumps-sham-voting-commission.com/#hassan")**
Our government is founded on the idea that everyone deserves a voice.
For decades, Americans have fought, marched, and spoken up for the idea that all citizens should have a say in our government. Simply put, the right to vote is at the center of our democracy.
Donald Trump and Washington Republicans are jeopardizing that right, and it is incumbent upon all of us to speak out.
[**If you believe that voter suppression has no place in America, add your name to call on Congress to disband Trump 's sham voting commission now.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fde69/2a380d31/1d6f8181/fe05b1/2564500261/VEsC/ "http://www.trumps-sham-voting-commission.com/#hassan")
Thanks for taking action,
Team Maggie
